title: Buttery Chewy Japanese Haricot Bean Ramen
description: A romantic, high-protein Japanese dish featuring haricot beans, buttery goodness, and a rich, chewy texture. Perfect for a cozy night in.
instructions: Drain and rinse the soaked haricot beans, then set aside.
In a large pot, combine the dashi broth, soy sauce, mirin, grated ginger, and garlic. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low.
In a large skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the haricot beans and cook until lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the cooked haricot beans to the pot with the dashi broth mixture.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter. Once melted, add the sesame oil and cook for 1 minute.
Add the ramen noodles to the skillet and cook, stirring constantly, until lightly browned and fragrant, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the cooked noodles to the pot with the dashi broth mixture.
Simmer the ramen mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together and the noodles are chewy.
Stir in the chopped green onions and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Serve the ramen hot, garnished with additional green onions if desired.
